en: Man, where do I start with this one? Because I have some stuff to say, and you all wanted to hear it.
ko: 야, 이건 어디서부터 시작해야 하지? 할 말이 좀 있는데, 너희가 다 듣고 싶어했잖아.

en: I saw the love from the last video. Anyways, was it just me, or were some of your closest friends made during lunchtime?
Because that was the time to talk, mess around, and not deal with any schoolwork.
ko: 왜냐면 그때가 떠들고, 장난치고, 공부 안 해도 되는 시간이었거든.

en: You were actually happy. What's your favorite subject, Jorge?
Lunch? So many things would happen during these 30 minutes.
ko: 이 30분 동안 정말 많은 일이 일어났어.

en: During middle school, I would sit with my buddies, Juan, Alexis, Harold, and several others at the school cafeteria.
ko: 중학교 때 학교 식당에서 친구들 Juan, Alexis, Harold이랑 다른 애들이랑 앉았어.

en: And we would talk about the dumbest things. Bro, do you brush your teeth before or after eating breakfast?
Wait, before. That's the first thing I do in the morning.
ko: "잠깐, 전에? 아침에 제일 먼저 하는 거야."

en: But you ruin your breakfast with all that minty breath. Don't tell me you brush your teeth after.
Yeah, we would start some dumb arguments. Our specialty, though, was yo mama jokes.
Man, yo mama so fat, I ran out of gas trying to drive around her.
ko: "야, 네 엄마 너무 뚱뚱해서 차로 돌아가다가 기름 떨어졌어."

en: Well, yo mama so ugly, she's the reason why your dad left.
ko: "네 엄마 너무 못생겨서 아빠가 떠난 거야."

en: You're right. Too far. On a lighter note, I would throw off my friends with certain magic tricks
that I knew back then as well. The most popular one was this quarter trick that I knew.
ko: 제일 인기 있었던 건 동전 마술이었어.

en: I already shared it before, but let me show you it again. All right, guys.
ko: 전에 한 번 보여줬었는데, 다시 한번 보여줄게.

en: Welcome back to Orchata Magic. When you guys see this quarter, I will make it move from one hand to the other.
ko: 이 동전을 한 손에서 다른 손으로 옮겨볼게요.

en: Here we go. Three, two, one.
Ooh, you guys saw that? One more time, one more time, one more time. Three, two, one.
Oh, let's do it really fast. Do it really fast. Three, two, one. Oh, dang it.
One more time, one more time, one more time. Ignore that, ignore that. Three, two, one.
All right, back to the story time. Bro, how did you do that?
I know how he did it. It's not that hard. Someone always had to say something.
ko: "나 걔가 어떻게 한 건지 알아. 별로 안 어려워."

en: And is it really a cafeteria story time without talking about the food?
ko: 그리고 급식 얘기 없이 식당 이야기가 되겠어?

en: Because there was such a big spectrum on the food reviews.
There are mainly three different kinds of people when they came to school food.
ko: 학교 급식에 대해서 크게 세 종류의 사람이 있었어.

en: The first were the ones who absolutely hated it. Wouldn't touch it, wouldn't eat it.
They would bring their own food. Ugh, that bread tastes like cardboard.
Picky eaters, am I right? The second were the ones who didn't mind it.
They would eat it and not complain. Eh, free food.
And the third were the ones who loved it. They loved it a little too much though because they would devour the food.
Hey, you gonna eat that chicken patty? My guy, I know the food isn't horrible, but you're different.
Yeah, the American public school system really gave us chocolate milk and burgers to eat at 11 in the morning.
ko: 응, 미국 공립학교가 진짜 아침 11시에 초코 우유랑 버거를 줬어.

en: Let me know if your school did or does the same thing. Every Friday, however, the school would give us pizza.
It was a tradition. And my buddies would bring takis, hot fries, really any chip that was spicy.
But at the same time, we would never admit that they were spicy.
ko: 하지만 동시에 맵다는 걸 절대 인정 안 했어.

en: Bro, these hot fries aren't even spicy to me.
ko: "야, 이 핫프라이 나한테는 하나도 안 매워."

en: Yeah, man, they're not hot. I know one of you guys is like that, manning up and saying they're not spicy.
Whatever, you're cool. We would then wash it down with strawberry milk.
Man, the stuff we would put in our bodies. Anyways, before we continue the story,
ko: 야, 우리 몸에 뭘 넣었던 거야.

en: I wanted to announce that I created a brand new channel, Horchata Clips.
ko: 어쨌든, 이야기를 계속하기 전에, 새로운 채널 Horchata Clips를 만들었다고 알려주고 싶어.

en: Yeah, I've been streaming a lot on Twitch and I decided to make a channel where I can post the stream highlights.
ko: 응, Twitch에서 방송을 많이 하고 있는데

en: You know, the best parts, story times, gaming, commentary, reactions, all the good stuff.
ko: 있잖아, 베스트 장면, 스토리 타임, 게임, 해설, 리액션, 좋은 것만 모아서.

en: Even talking to you guys. So subscribe, you don't wanna miss it.
I'm gonna put my heart and soul into this new channel.
ko: 이 새 채널에 전심전력을 다할 거야.

en: Anyways, back to the crazy stuff that would happen. This one time, me and my buddies
were chilling in the cafeteria like any normal afternoon where out of nowhere,
ko: 한번은 나랑 친구들이 평범한 오후처럼 식당에서 놀고 있었는데, 갑자기 학교 직원 아줌마 한 분이 우리 테이블로 달려왔어.

en: one of the school staff ladies just rushes to our table.
ko: 한번은 나랑 친구들이 평범한 오후처럼 식당에서 놀고 있었는데, 갑자기 학교 직원 아줌마 한 분이 우리 테이블로 달려왔어.

en: How can you guys do something like that? I need your name so I can write you guys up.
Wait, what? Look at Aaron, he's crying and he pointed at you guys.
ko: "Aaron 좀 봐, 울고 있고 너희를 가리켰어!"

en: And none of us ever seen that kid in our lives.
ko: 우리 중 아무도 그 애를 본 적이 없었어.

en: And on my mom, we didn't do anything because we didn't even know he existed.
ko: 진짜 맹세하는데, 우리는 아무것도 안 했어. 걔가 존재하는지도 몰랐으니까.

en: We were so confused and it took so long for the staff lady to believe us.
ko: 우리가 너무 당황했고 직원 아줌마가 우리 말을 믿는 데 한참 걸렸어.

en: Well, I'm gonna keep an eye on you guys. Let's go, Aaron.
Um, what was that? What just happened? I remember turning red from embarrassment
because it just felt like a fever dream. Also, would it really be a school lunchtime without somebody making a beat
ko: 그리고 누군가 비트를 만들고 점심 테이블에서 프리스타일하는 사람 없이 학교 점심시간이 되겠어?

en: and freestyling at the lunch table? All right, so let's go do your thing.
Mm. That's it? That's all he did.
ko: 그게 다야? 걔가 한 게 그게 다야?

en: That's all he got. He lose tempo from what? Oh, the pressure of the camera?
Hey. Hey. Lunch was lit.
Lunch was crazy, bro. Every kid, every single kid in that frickin' lunchroom, there were 200 kids in there.
No music. Pfft, pfft, pfft, pfft, pfft, pfft, pfft, pfft, pfft, pfft.
And the vice principal could not do anything about it.
ko: 그리고 교감 선생님이 어떻게 할 수가 없었어.

en: He could not do anything about it. I got him telling story times now.
They would just be smacking on the table, bro.
ko: 걔네가 그냥 테이블을 두드리고 있었어, 진짜.

en: Oh my god. That's so funny. Yeah, we had nothing better to do
because there'd always be that one kid rapping over the beat and homie would show off.
ko: 응, 할 게 없었거든. 항상 그 한 명이 있었어 비트 위에서 랩하는, 걔가 자랑했어.

en: Ooh, we're gonna get out the hood with this one. Teachers would get so mad since we would be so loud.
Everybody say such and then we keep it going. Eggs, bacon, grits.
Everybody, may I have your attention please? My buddy Victor, he's single.
Again, we were so immature, but there was only one thing that would get more attention than us
and that was the cafeteria fights. They wouldn't be too frequent, but when they occurred,
it felt like we were watching a pay-per-view boxing. All right, ladies and gentlemen,
ko: 자주 있진 않았지만, 일어나면 유료 권투 시합 보는 것 같았어.

en: in this corner, standing at three foot 10, we have Jamal.
ko: "자, 신사 숙녀 여러분, 이 코너에 키 117cm, Jamal입니다!"

en: Hey, let's go, Jamal, let's go.
And in this corner, standing at three foot nine from homeroom 208, we have Marcus.
Man, why you gotta talk to my girl? I mean, is she really your girl if she's talking to me?
ko: "야, 왜 내 여자한테 말 거는 거야?"

en: Ooh, fight, fight, fight, fight.
All righty, guys, let's get ready to rumble.
And this one fight in particular, my guy Jamal threw a milk carton on Marcus and it spilled all over him.
ko: 특히 이 싸움에서 Jamal이 Marcus한테 우유갑을 던졌는데 온몸에 쏟아졌어.

en: Ooh, it's getting messy. Me and my boys will be sitting on our lunch table trying to get a better view.
And shout out to the cameraman as well because they act like they're recording the fight,
ko: 그리고 카메라맨한테도 감사, 싸움 찍는 척하는데 실제로는 폰이 바닥을 향하고 있고,

en: but in reality, their phone is aiming at the floor and the staff arrives so late to break it up
ko: 직원들은 말리러 너무 늦게 와서, 올 때쯤이면 점심 종이 울리고

en: because by the time they arrive, the lunch bell rings and we have to run back to class.
Everybody, run. Ah, I can't be late, I can't be late.
Yeah, we would just leave the crime scene. Ah, those are some good times.
ko: 응, 우리는 그냥 범죄 현장을 떠났어.

en: Cling, cling, cling, cling. Yes, sir, again, subscribe to the brand new channel.
My downstairs neighbors are probably really pissed right now because you can hear that, I'm pretty sure.
ko: 아래층 이웃이 지금 진짜 화났을 거야. 들렸을 거 확실해.
